metadata.subject: Applied Design, Skills and Technologies
metadata.description: Students explore food science, cultural foodways, and sustainable food systems, examining how food connects to identity, health, environment, and community. This course emphasizes critical thinking about food production, distribution, and consumption patterns.
metadata.general_description: An applied course exploring food science, cultural traditions, and sustainable food systems.

big_ideas.statement: Food is a fundamental human need that connects to health, culture, identity, and sustainability.
big_ideas.elaboration: Understanding food systems holistically reveals interconnections between agriculture, nutrition, environment, economy, and social well-being.

big_ideas.statement: Cultural foodways reflect values, traditions, and histories while providing strategies for health, community, and sustainability.
big_ideas.elaboration: Food practices are culturally specific and carry meaning beyond nutrition, connecting people to heritage, place, and community.

big_ideas.statement: Food science explains how ingredients and cooking methods affect nutrition, safety, taste, and quality.
big_ideas.elaboration: Scientific understanding of food composition and preparation enables informed choices and innovative food solutions.

big_ideas.statement: Sustainable food systems balance environmental, economic, and social considerations to ensure long-term viability and equity.
big_ideas.elaboration: Addressing food security and sustainability requires examining production practices, distribution systems, and consumption patterns globally and locally.

competencies.raw: Design food systems approaches that promote health, culture, and sustainability
i_can_statements: I can develop community food programs that address food security
I can design sustainable food production and distribution models
I can create culturally appropriate food initiatives
I can plan food systems that minimize environmental impact

competencies.raw: Plan food-based projects that explore cultural traditions and food science
i_can_statements: I can design food projects exploring cultural foodways and traditions
I can develop experiments investigating food science principles
I can plan food preservation and preparation projects
I can create educational food activities and demonstrations

competencies.raw: Develop approaches to address food security, access, and equity issues
i_can_statements: I can analyze food security challenges in communities
I can design programs to increase food access and affordability
I can create advocacy campaigns for food justice
I can develop strategies for reducing food waste and loss

competencies.raw: Evaluate food systems for sustainability and impacts on health and environment
i_can_statements: I can assess environmental impacts of food production and consumption
I can analyze food security and equity in different communities
I can evaluate sustainability of food systems and practices
I can identify opportunities for systemic food system improvements

competencies.raw: Conduct food science investigations and experiments
i_can_statements: I can design and conduct food science experiments
I can investigate how cooking affects food composition
I can analyze nutritional content of foods
I can explore food preservation methods and principles

competencies.raw: Research and document cultural food traditions and foodways
i_can_statements: I can research food practices in different cultures
I can document traditional food preparation methods
I can identify cultural significance of foods and food practices
I can create resources celebrating cultural foodways

competencies.raw: Apply food preservation and preparation techniques
i_can_statements: I can preserve foods using traditional and modern methods
I can prepare foods using culturally significant techniques
I can work with whole ingredients and local seasonal foods
I can demonstrate food preparation from diverse culinary traditions

competencies.raw: Analyze and communicate about food systems and sustainability
i_can_statements: I can analyze food production and distribution systems
I can trace food sources and supply chains
I can communicate findings about food systems and sustainability
I can advocate for sustainable and equitable food practices

competencies.raw: Use tools and equipment for food science investigations
i_can_statements: I can use laboratory equipment for food testing
I can operate food preparation equipment
I can use food preservation equipment and techniques
I can maintain and care for scientific and kitchen equipment

competencies.raw: Use digital tools to research food systems and sustainability
i_can_statements: I can access databases and resources about food systems
I can use mapping and visualization tools for food system analysis
I can analyze food data using spreadsheets and charts
I can create digital presentations about food topics

competencies.raw: Apply technology to track and communicate food sustainability
i_can_statements: I can use tools to calculate carbon footprints of foods
I can document and share sustainable food practices
I can use digital platforms for community food initiatives
I can access information about local and sustainable food sources

competencies.raw: Evaluate emerging food technologies and their impacts
i_can_statements: I can assess impacts of food technologies on sustainability
I can understand genetic modification and food innovation
I can evaluate food traceability and transparency technologies
I can identify both benefits and concerns of emerging food technologies

areas.name: Food Science and Nutrition
topics: Food composition: macronutrients, micronutrients, phytochemicals
How cooking affects food: chemical and physical changes
Food safety: contamination, preservation, storage
Nutrition and health relationships
Digestive processes and nutrient absorption
Special diets and nutritional needs of different populations

areas.name: Cultural Foodways and Food Traditions
topics: First Peoples food practices and traditional knowledge
Food traditions across cultures and communities
Food as cultural identity and expression
Traditional food preparation methods
Food and celebration: rituals and significance
Contemporary interpretations of traditional foodways

areas.name: Food Systems and Sustainability
topics: Food production: agriculture, aquaculture, hunting, gathering
Food distribution and supply chains
Environmental impacts of food systems
Food security and access issues
Sustainable agriculture and food production practices
Local food systems and community food security

areas.name: Food Policy and Advocacy
topics: Food security and food justice issues
Food policy and regulation
Labeling and transparency in food systems
Fair trade and ethical food sourcing
Food waste reduction and strategies
Community food initiatives and advocacy movements
